claim: Pilot the knowledge standard's CONVERT runbook on a real existing store - SECOND-BRAIN/second-brain (a markdown-canonical assertion graph with [[wikilink]] frontmatter). Run standard/CONVERT.md end to end: inventory, add the frontmatter spine, transform [[wikilink]] refs to path-links + deps ids, decide tier per doc (default OKF floor), generate the manifest, and validate green. The output proves the standard converts a non-trivial store without losing provenance, and exercises the wikilink->path/deps transform CONVERT names but no conformance fixture covers yet.

evidence.detail: Identified as the natural first real-world test of the standard after building it plus the conformance suite. SECOND-BRAIN/second-brain was named in the originating session as the closest existing precedent (markdown-canonical + derived index) and cleanest pilot. Verbatim thread: document:meta/2026-06-21-okf-cb-session-source.md. First belief in the knowledge: graph, no conflict.

claim: Apply the threads:[] provenance convention (standard/types.md) across all existing knowledge-repo docs, not only the two meta docs seeded in the originating session. Every doc authored in a session should carry threads:[<origin-id>, ...]; backfill meta:okf-session-2026-06-21 onto the standard/, demo/, and remaining meta/ docs the session produced, and add a `## Provenance` body table where per-touch detail is wanted. Makes the convention actually-applied rather than only-specified.

evidence.detail: The threads convention was defined in standard/types.md and seeded only on meta/okf-vs-cb-layering.md and meta/two-tier-knowledge-architecture.md; the rest of the repo it produced is unbackfilled. Verbatim thread: document:meta/2026-06-21-okf-cb-session-source.md.

claim: knowledge/beliefs/ is the methodology's own CB belief graph (beliefs.json + a CB graph manifest), NOT an OKF bundle. Never run the OKF tooling (mix knowledge.manifest / mix knowledge.validate) against it: the validator reports it stale because it has zero .md docs, and the manifest generator would overwrite its CB graph descriptor with an empty OKF manifest. It is read and written only through the CB graph tooling (mix bs, the cb.preflight/import write flow). Per standard/KNOWLEDGE.md section 1.1 (the standard owns format rules; the consumer owns operational directives), this guard is consumer-owned and lives here in the graph, not duplicated as prose in CLAUDE.md.

evidence.detail: Surfaced during the repo audit: validate.py/knowledge.validate on beliefs/ reports manifest_stale, and build_manifest/knowledge.manifest on it produces {count:0, docs:[]}, clobbering the CB descriptor {depends_on, namespace, description}. # CLAUDE.md - okf/ (the OKF integration extension)

This directory is the Knowledge methodology: a portable OKF-based format for knowledge across all my repos and productivity domains, with Composable Beliefs as an opt-in rigor ceiling. It is CB's OKF integration extension, folded into composable-beliefs beside the tooling that implements it (it used to be the standalone `knowledge` repo).

render_sections.title: The okfx graph is not an OKF bundle

invariants: okf/CLAUDE.md is read-only - edits are overwritten on next generation
Every line of output traces to exactly one belief's claim field
deps equals the union of all beliefs referenced in render_sections
Section titles are human-readable; tags remain free for cross-cutting audit queries

evidence.detail: The okfx: output-target contract that makes okf/CLAUDE.md a graph-compiled, --check-gated output-target, discharging okfx:a004. Mirrors the framework's cb:c065 (output:claude-md) shape; deps equal the union of the render_sections' beliefs (okfx:a003 the beliefs/ guard, plus okfx:a006-a009). Compiled by mix cb.generate.claude_md with the new --beliefs PATH override pointing the store at okf/beliefs.json.

Split out of okfx:a004 deliberately to keep that task to the compile machinery and out of belief-prose path migration, the same boundary that kept the tooling rename separate.

This directory is the Knowledge methodology: a portable OKF-based format for knowledge across all my repos and productivity domains, with Composable Beliefs as an opt-in rigor ceiling. It is CB's OKF integration extension, folded into composable-beliefs beside the tooling that implements it (it used to be the standalone `knowledge` repo).
